tldr: Move human/team password vault entries to a cloud-native, Azure AD-controlled secrets store. Best fit when your apps already run on Azure and you want machine-grade audit, rotation, and RBAC instead of a shared password manager.
why_switch: Native Azure AD (Entra ID) RBAC and Conditional Access on every secret read
Programmatic access via Azure SDK with managed identities — no static API tokens to distribute
Built-in versioning, soft-delete, and purge protection for secrets and keys
HSM-backed key storage (Premium tier) for compliance workloads
Per-secret pricing scales down for small workloads
what_to_export: API keys and tokens stored as 1Password items
Service-account credentials referenced by 1Password Connect or CLI
Environment variable groups consumed by pipelines
Vault/folder structure (to model as Key Vault naming prefixes or separate vaults)
Access groups and permissions (to map to Azure RBAC role assignments)
export_method: Use 1Password desktop app: File > Export > 1PUX (or unencrypted JSON for scripting). For programmatic extraction, use the 1Password CLI (`op item list` / `op item get`) to dump items as JSON. Strip non-secret fields before import.
import_method: Create a Key Vault, then use Azure CLI (`az keyvault secret set`) or the Azure SDK (`@azure/keyvault-secrets`) in a script that iterates over the exported JSON. For certificates use `az keyvault certificate import`. Assign RBAC roles (Key Vault Secrets User/Officer) to the principals that need access.

concept_mapping.note: One Key Vault per environment or trust boundary is common
Multi-field 1Password items must be flattened or stored as JSON string
Store small payloads as base64 secrets or move binaries to Azure Blob
Tags are queryable on listing operations
Assign at vault or secret scope via Entra ID principals
Each `set` creates a new immutable version
Prefer managed identities for Azure-hosted workloads
Use the Certificates API, not Secrets, to keep cert lifecycle features

effort: 1-3 weeks for a team of 5-20 developers, depending on number of integrations and pipelines that reference 1Password Connect/CLI.
gotchas: Secret names in Key Vault are restricted to alphanumerics and hyphens — rename items during import
Multi-field 1Password items lose structure unless serialized to JSON in the secret value
Soft-delete and purge-protection are irreversible once enabled — decide policy before bulk import
Per-operation pricing means tight loops on `getSecret` can add up; cache where safe
Browser autofill and end-user password sharing have no Key Vault equivalent — keep 1Password for human secrets if needed
Access is Azure AD only; non-Azure workloads need service principals with client secrets or federated credentials

email.subject: Moving our shared secrets from 1Password to Azure Key Vault
email.body:
Hi team,

We're migrating machine-readable secrets (API keys, connection strings, certs, CI tokens) from 1Password to Azure Key Vault so apps and pipelines authenticate via Azure AD/managed identities instead of long-lived 1Password tokens.

Plan: export current items to 1PUX/JSON, script the import into a per-environment Key Vault, and switch services over one integration at a time. Human-only items (browser logins, personal passwords) stay in 1Password.

Expect 1-3 weeks. I'll share the cutover schedule and the new RBAC matrix shortly. Reply if you own an integration that reads from 1Password Connect or the CLI so we can sequence it.

Thanks.
